Katharina Klug and her expert guests, Daniel and Sander, provide a wealth of knowledge and practical advice for leveraging data to achieve business success. Tune in to discover how embedded analytics can support your business processes and decision-making capabilities.

Click here and tune in now!

 

Do you want to contact us or send your suggestion for the next episodes? Send us an e-mail to insides4@sap.com

 

Podcast Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud

There is no customer success without project success and product success! Leverage the unique knowledge and expertise of SAP S/4HANA Cloud experts, partners, and customers, sharing their SAP S/4HANA Cloud product insights, implementation project best practices and lessons learned – anywhere, anytime. In this podcast, we give you all ingredients to get to the next level and make your SAP S/4HANA Cloud project a success. Check all the episodes at sap.com/podcasts, Spotify, Apple Podcasts, TuneIn, and more audio platforms.

entry.title: An Introduction to Documentation Versions on the SAP Help Portal for Functional Localization
entry.content:
I’m pleased to share the latest information on accessing documentation on the SAP Help Portal for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. In this blog post, I’ll explain how you can choose the right documentation version for your technical requirements and discuss the recent updates on the SAP Help Portal for maintenance phases.

Title image for the topic of documentation versions on the SAP Help Portal

I’ll be focusing today on the country/region-specific documentation for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition that is published through the Documentation Delivery with SAP Notes process, though there are useful tips here for everyone who browses version-specific content on the SAP Help Portal.

1. Choosing the Right Documentation Version for You

The SAP Help Portal is your number-one resource for information on SAP products. When navigating documentation, it’s important to verify that the version you’re browsing is correct for your solution. You can do this by checking the Version Selector drop-down menu, as shown in this example from the Local Versions page:

Drop-down version selection on the SAP Help Portal

Why do we recommend this version verification step? Well, you can access SAP Help Portal documentation by many different means, such as links from SAP Notes and What’s New topics, as well as from search engines. You may be directed to the highest version, which is not necessarily the same version implemented by your company. Different versions may have different scope functionality described in the documentation, so it’s best to always check this drop-down menu when accessing the SAP Help Portal.

2. New on the SAP Help Portal: Maintenance Information

You might have noticed that, when you access documentation for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, there’s a new information banner that specifies the maintenance phase:

Maintenance message now available on the SAP Help Portal

This information banner differentiates between two maintenance phases, Mainstream Maintenance and Extended Maintenance. If you are an SAP customer or partner with a valid SAP S-User ID, you can refer to the SAP Maintenance Phases resource for further details on this topic.

For the most recent documentation versions on the SAP Help Portal, you are informed that “documentation is updated with the support package stacks”. However, there is an important exception to this rule for country/region-specific updates delivered for functional localization.

3. Always Up-to-Date: Documentation Delivery for Functional Localization

For country/region-specific development delivered for functional localization, such as legal changes, a new process provides documentation updates between support package stacks. As detailed in the recent blog post on Documentation Delivery on the SAP Help Portal with SAP Notes, you now have access to SAP Help Portal documentation that reflects the latest information, regardless of the planned support package release dates.  

For both the mainstream and extended maintenance phases of SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, we’re glad to provide you with up-to-date information on how SAP meets the local legal requirement for your solution. 

To find more information on country/region-specific topics, I recommend the Localization topic page on the SAP Community. There you can find the latest news, resources, and upcoming events for SAP’s localized solutions. Remember, too, that you can always select the Create a Question option on the Localization topic page to engage with experts and resolve any doubts you may have:

Create a Question for Localization on the SAP Community

And for those of you who use SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, you can find more details on the SAP Help Portal and SAP Community in our dedicated blog series:

  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Navigating the SAP Help Portal
  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Engaging with the SAP Community

That’s it for today, though please let me know in the comments section if you have any questions. Your feedback is always appreciated.

Thank you and until next time,

Barnabas

entry.title: What is new in S/4HANA Manufacturing for Production Engineering & Operations (PEO) in 2023 FPS2
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Release 2023 FPS2 for S/4HANA Manufacturing for production engineering and operations (PEO) offers a range of new features and enhancements designed to improve efficiency and flexibility in production engineering and manufacturing processes. This blog post explores the key innovations in the areas of production engineering, production operations, concurrent engineering, and configuration management.

Production Engineering

Logical Components for Unitized Products

In the Manage Shop Floor Routings app for products with unitized parameter effectivity, you can use logical components if you need to assign alternative components to operation activities that are otherwise identical. This can save you time and effort as you don't need to split the operation activity (OA). This results in more efficient shop floor routing maintenance for operation activities that have different components for different effectivity ranges.

So instead of having to create a separate operation activity for each component, as was the case in previous releases:

 

You now only need to create one operation activity with a logical component that acts as a placeholder for the components used in each model-unit effectivity range:

For more information see here: How to Avoid Activity Splits for Alternative Components with Different Effectivity Ranges

Simplification of Production Version for Unitized Products

Handling of the production version is now simplified for products with unitized parameter effectivity to provide you with more transparency. Only one valid production version exists at a time, which means:

  • There is no need to display a Version Details table.
  • When you create a new version of the MBOM and shop floor routing and update the production version, the system overwrites the existing production version with the new one.
  • No valid-from date is required: the production version is always valid from the creation date.

For more information see here: Understanding the Version Details Table

Usability Enhancements for Work Instructions

When you create work instructions in the Manage Shop Floor Routings app, you can now display and change the details of an inline hyperlink reference, which means you can easily identify the object to which the hyperlink refers, and you can also change the display text if you need to add more clarity.

For more information see here : How to Edit References in Work Instructions

You can also automatically generate a table of contents for work instructions to enable seamless navigation between different sections of large-scale work instructions – saving time for both the work instruction author and the production operator. This navigation pane is also available for standard texts.

 

For more information see here: How to Create Bookmarks in Work Instructions

Production Operations

Rework Handling in the Order-Specific Shop Floor Routing

If, as a result of a defect, the quality engineer has inserted a rework operation into the routing structure, it is now possible to change rework operations and operation activities in the order-specific shop floor routing. This means that you can edit operation activities, as long as they have not yet been started in execution.

You can also perform the following activities, if necessary, resulting in greater flexibility in the event of order changes:

  • Assign components to the rework operation activities.
  • Add/remove work instructions, inspection characteristics, PRTs, buyoffs, qualifications, and so on, to/from rework operation activities, as part of a follow-up order change.

For more information see here: Performing Unplanned Rework

Enhanced Serial Number Group Processing

Several new features improve the execution of serial numbers in mass processing mode.

In the serial number group, you can now also include operation activities that contain batch-managed and quantity-based traceable components. This allows the assembly of batch-managed and quantity-based components within one order or across several orders.

When creating a defect for a serial number group, you can now add an attachment to upload pictures, photographs, and documents related to the defect. These attachments are then also available in the Process Defects app where they can be viewed by the production or quality engineer to provide visual support for assessing the situation.

When you complete or skip a serial number group, the system now automatically gives you the option to copy the group so that you can continue to process it at the next available operation activity. This makes the process more efficient when you want to continue processing all serial numbers together.

For more information see here: Process Serial Number Groups

Enhancements to the As-Built Report

Several new features guarantee accurate traceability information and provide capabilities to reconcile the As-Built Report with the actual production state of a product.

You can now add a component to the as-built information that was initially planned in the production order and that has been physically assembled in execution but for which no data collection has been performed. This might happen, for example, if the production operator on the shop floor simply forgot to record the data in the Perform Operation Activity app.

You can also add a component to the as-built information that was never a part of the production order (unplanned), but which has been physically assembled in execution, in order to reflect rework or an ad-hoc replacement, for example.

You can also configure a reason code and note when you add a component. Furthermore, these newly added components are not only added to the as-built information but are also available in the other apps for monitoring production, including the Order Genealogy and Product Genealogy, as well as the Production Action Log providing all the necessary tracking information.

For more information see here: As-Built Report

Printing in PEO

Printing is now possible from a wide variety of apps in PEO. This is supported by Adobe templates, which you can configure to define the format and content of the output according to your requirements.

You can now print operation activities, including their work instructions, from the following four apps:

  • My Work Queue
  • Perform Operation Activity
  • Manage Production Orders
  • Manage Production Operations

For more information see here: How to Print Operation Activities in Manage Production Operations

You can also print product genealogy and order genealogy from the Product Genealogy and Order Genealogy apps.

For more information see here: How to Print the Order Genealogy  

End User Extensibility

In the Perform Operation Activity app, you can use app extensibility to add your own custom columns in the interactive work instructions table. You can also add action buttons for your own custom actions. These features allow you to provide your production operators with exactly the information and capabilities they need.

To make result recording more efficient based on your needs, you can also add additional columns to inspection characteristics using the app extensibility in the Perform Operation Activity app.

For more information see here: App Extensibility: Perform Operation Activity (Expert)

Concurrent Engineering

Handling of BOM Maturity in EBOM-Driven Scenario

BOM maturity levels provide enhanced flexibility and collaboration between product design and production engineering. The production engineer can get a head start with planning activities, such as MRP, even when the product design is not fully complete. In this release, it is now possible to assign maturity levels to EBOMs.

The Change Impact Analysis now detects changes in BOM maturity levels, and you can control how MBOMs inherit the BOM maturity level during the EBOM-to-MBOM handover in the Visual Enterprise Manufacturing Planner (VEMP).

For more information see here: Handling of BOM Maturity Levels  

Configuration Management (Variant Configuration)

Configure-to-Order (CTO): Improved Handling of Object Dependencies in VEMP

A variety of new features in VEMP enable you to manage the impact of changes in variant configuration during the EBOM-to-MBOM handover.

  • It is possible to display information on object dependencies for both the source EBOM and the target MBOM in the source, working, and target panels of VEMP.
  • VEMP adjusts object dependencies if you restructure the target MBOM during the EBOM-to-MBOM handover.
  • You can maintain local object dependencies for BOM items in your target MBOM in the working panel by navigating to the Dependency Maintenance Table (DMT) on the Web UI.
  • Variant configuration in PEO now supports both local and global object dependencies. You cannot create a global object dependency in VEMP, but you can assign an existing global object dependency to an MBOM item.
  • It is possible to reconcile object dependencies by comparing differences between the object dependencies in the source EBOM and the object dependencies in the target MBOM and making decisions on how to handle them.
  • You can implement a BAdI to handle object dependencies in cases where object dependencies exist at multiple levels of the source EBOM hierarchy and where using the Plan-as-Designed function is not a viable option.

For more information see here: Handling of Object Dependencies

Engineer-to-Order (ETO): New App for Managing ETO Production Models

A new app, Manage Production Models – Engineer-to-Order, provides enhanced flexibility when implementing customer-specific requirements. You can use this app to create and maintain engineer-to-order (ETO) production models for configurable materials. That is, you have created and configured a sales order item for a particular customer but the standard configuration options in the sales order do not cover all the customer's requirements. Therefore, you still need to make some additional customer-specific adjustments to the MBOM and shop floor routing to cover this customer's specific needs.

The production model provides a joint view of the manufacturing bill of material (MBOM) and the shop floor routing and offers an alternative way of handling BOM components and shop floor production steps: side-by-side in one application.

  • Based on the 150% configure-to-order production model, you can create an ETO production model (which consists of an ETO version BOM and ETO shop floor routing).
  • You can enhance the ETO production model by adding, removing, or modifying BOM components or production steps, for example.
  • You can use the ETO production model instead of the generic CTO production model to create the production order for a sales order item.

For more information see here: Manage Production Models - Engineer-to-Order

Want to Find Out More?

You can find more information on all new features provided in SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for production engineering and operations (PEO) Release 2023 FPS2 here:

  • What's New | SAP Help Portal – just go to Manufacturing for Production Engineering and Operations

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Send PDF with XML file via email to your business partners
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing options for Brazil allows you to send DANFE (NF-e) and DACTE (CT-e) in PDF format to your business partners via email. For SAP S/4HANA and SAP ERP, you use the NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) transaction to display the distribution status of the XML and PDF files for each electronic fiscal document.

Take a look at the updates in our What's New session.

Instructions to generate and submit the PDF with the XML file:

1 - Follow the manual instructions in SAP Note 3532295 to customize the attached form template (template.xdp) using the Adobe LiveCycle Designer. The file is a sample that can be used to create the template for the DANFE (NF-e). The maximum template size is set to 2 MB; remember to resize or compress images used in the file.

Editing the sample file in Adobe LiveCycle Designer.

2 - After creating a new template as an XDP file in the Adobe LiveCycle Designer, use a tool to encode the file to Base64 and save it to your computer. Since an XDP file has an XML format, you can copy its source code using a simple text editor before encoding it.

The content of an XDP file opened in a text editor.

3 - You must use an HTTP client or requests via curl to update the template in the service. In the following instructions, an HTTP client is used to create the requests (GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE) and set the endpoint to the URL https://nfe.cfapps.br10.hana.ondemand.com. Refer to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub for further details.

3.1 – The following process relies on token authentication (also known as bearer authentication), which is used over HTTPS (SSL). This authentication allows you to access URLs of our service. The client must send the token in the authorization header when making requests to protected resources:

 

Authorization: Bearer <token>

 

For more details about token authentication in the SAP BTP, check the following pages:

  • OAuth JWT Bearer Authentication
  • OAuth 2.0 – Understanding Token Exchange

3.2 - In the HTTP client, create a POST request with the following details:

  • URL: https://nfe.cfapps.br10.hana.ondemand.com/pdf-service/v1/templates
  • Method: POST
  • Auth: Input the generated authentication token to communicate with our cloud services. Use your clientId and clientSecret to get the token in the SAP BTP.

3.3 – In the Body, you must send the content using the HTTP client, filling in the following details:

  • Template name in templateName field, which can be whatever you want.
  • Electronic document type in documentType field, which must be filled in with “NFE” for DANFE or “CTE” for DACTE.
  • Action type in actionType field, filled in with “AUTHORIZE”.
  • Country in country field, filled in with “BR”.
  • Electronic document layout in the version field; this is an optional field, and in the example, it is filled in with “4.00”, which is the current NF-e (DANFE) layout version.
  • Environment Type in environmentType field; this is an optional field, and it is filled in with “HOMOLOGATION” for testing purposes, or “PRODUCTION”.
  • The content of the XDP template, encoded to Base64, must be pasted into xdpTemplate field.

 

{
	"templateName": "Name of the template",
	"documentType": "NFE",
	"actionType": "AUTHORIZE",
	"country": "BR",
	"version": "4.00",
	"environmentType": "HOMOLOGATION",
	"xdpTemplate":
"Content of the template encoded to Base64 here"
}

 

Template update using an HTTP client.

Note: There is a restriction for documentType, actionType, country, version, and environmentType; you should not send two or more templates that have the same values in these fields.

4 - Open the following pages in SAP Help to understand how to update the email addresses of your business partners and confirm that jobs are scheduled in your backend system.

  • Configuring Email Addresses to Send XML and PDF Files
  • Scheduling Jobs for NF-e, CT-e, NFS-e

5 - Submit an NF-e document to your business partner in the NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) transaction. When the document is authorized by SEFAZ, the Distribution Log column will be updated.

6 - Click on the icon in the Distribution Log column to check the details of the email sent in Distribution History > Log. For more details, check the Distribution Bounce Statuses page in SAP Help.

Steps in the NF-e/CT-e Monitor (J1BNFE) transaction.

The email with the PDF and XML files will be received by your business partner.

Note: After updating the template, it may take up to 15 minutes for the new version to be used, after which your business partners will receive the new PDF version and XML file via email.

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the component LOD-LH-NFE (Outbound invoicing option for Brazil); just follow the instructions in SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

Use the regulatory change manager tool to find out if a solution to a legal change is planned. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool.

Thank you, and until next time,
Thiago


Tags: brasil, einvoice, einvoicing, e-invoicing, electronic invoice, electronic invoicing, electronic document, electronic documents, eletronic document, edocument, edoc, e-document, e-docs, e-doc, e-docs, drc, document compliance, nota fiscal eletrônica, eletronica, electronic nota fiscal, factura electrónica, conhecimento de transporte eletrônico, nf-e, ct-e

entry.title: Credit Management for Advanced Execution Order Item via Adhoc Billing
entry.content:
This blog will help you to understand how Adhoc Billing takes place in advanced execution order item.

In the billing solution adapted for S/4 Hana Service Transactions, known as Service Orders (SO) will be via Billing via Billing Plan After Release (Adhoc Billing). During this process user must create the bill plan.

Once the billing plan is added and service order is released, it will qualify for Credit Commitment update, Hence credit management integration process needs to be triggered, Following are the key categories where credit exposures movement takes place. Transaction code to view Credit Exposures: “UKM_COMMITMENTS”.

Type of Credit Exposures Category

Description of Credit Exposures Category

110

Service Order & Service Contract

410

Service Confirmations

500

Billing Document Value

200

Open Invoices

There are different billing relevance in service order for which credit management get triggered.

In this blog we will focusing more “Billing via Billing Plan After Release”.

Customizing for credit check:

SPRO---Service--- Transactions---- Basic Settings----- Credit Management----

Make sure the transaction type is enabled for credit check.

Make sure the service order item category is also credit check enable.

To create the service order user must assigned the service manager role to their user: SAP_BR_SERVICE_MANAGER.

Once the role is assigned to the user profile. Manage Service Order’s app can be accessible

Create the service order with advanced execution order item and add the billing relevance as :“Billing via Billing Plan After Release”.

Once the user added the bill relevance as “Billing via Billing Plan After Release”,Bill Plan needs to be added manually to the service order item. Here user can add the bill plan via billing percentage as well.

After creating the service order, user must manually release the item. On released action credit check process is getting triggered to check whether the user is having sufficient credit to create the new service order or to release the item. If the credit check is not successful, then service order will go into DCD case (Documented Credit Decisions). In this case user must go via approval process to get rid of the DCD blocker. Only authorized person can approve the DCD case for blocked service order depends upon the business.To approve the DCD, user should assign the below business role to their user profile.

SAP_BR_CREDIT_CONTROLLER

Once the role is assigned user can access the Manage Documented Credit Decisions” app. Go to the “Manage Documented Credit decisions” app.

Enter the service order and click on release to approve the service order.Once the service order is approved then item will set it in “RELEASED” status.

Further integration with the Credit Management process gets trigger and update the credit exposure commitments in the 110 (Service Order & Service Contract). 

To check the credit exposures: Run the transaction UKM_COMMITMENTS. Or go the Fiori app “Display Credit Exposure”. To access the” Display Credit Exposure” application user must have “Credit Controller” role assigned to their user. Enter the details as shown below. In this case Business Partner is “17100001” and credit segment is “1000”. Click on execute

On successful execution, Credit commitment data will be updated in the 110 category.

Create the BDR for service order 8000050264. To create the BDR, User should follow the below path:

There were two billing plans added to the service order item page. Hence two BDR’s got created.

Same can be view in transaction history of service order page.

Post the Invoice via VF01.

Post the second Invoice.

Check the credit commitment exposures in 500 category. Newly created invoices as shown in below image. The commitment value : Net Value + Taxes.

Next step is to Release the Billing Document (Invoice) to accounting. Go to VF02. Enter the Billing Document. Click on Release to Accounting

Once the billing documents are Released to Accounting , 200 category is getting updated with the credit commitment for 90010685 and 90010686

Once the credit commitment update is moved to 200 category. No exposures remain in 500 category.

In advanced execution of a service order, credit management plays a crucial role in ensuring financial discipline and customer satisfaction.

The Adhoc billing feature in this process flow is explained in detail how the credit management work in advanced execution order item in service order.

To know more about credit management: Kindly go through the below blog:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/credit-management-for-advanced-execution-order-item-dp92/ba-p/13973490

entry.title: Credit Management for Advanced Execution Order Item in Financial and Contract Accounting (FICA)
entry.content:
This blog will help you to understand how business process works when it comes to the financial and contract accounting scenario in advanced execution order item.

In the billing solution adapted for S/4 Hana Service Transactions, known as Service Orders (SO) will be via Dynamic Item Processor Framework (DIP) [transaction DP92], During this process a Debit Memo Request(DMR) document is generated. The DMR that is generated out of DIP could qualify for Credit Commitment update, Hence Credit management integration process needs to be triggered, Following are the key categories where credit exposures movement takes place.Transaction code to view Credit Exposures: “UKM_COMMITMENTS”. 

Type of Credit Exposures Category

Description of Credit Exposures Category

110

Service Order & Service Contract

410

Service Confirmations

500

Billing Document Value

200

Open Invoices

There are different billing relevance in service order for which credit management gets triggered.

In this blog we will be focusing  on how credit management process takes place in FI-CA enabled system. Behaviour is the same for summarized and itemized billing relevance. In this blog will be learning the end to end  process via summarized billing relevance.

Customizing for credit check:

SPRO---Service--- Transactions---- Basic Settings----- Credit Management----

Make sure the transaction type is credit check enabled.

Make sure the service order item category is also credit check enabled.

Check the DIP Profile Setting: Transaction “ODP1” .

In FICA enabled system, Steps to activate the FICA Integration:

Summarized Billing Relevance:

To create the service order user must assigned the service manager role to their user: SAP_BR_SERVICE_MANAGER.

Once the role is assigned to the user profile. Manage Service Order’s app can be accessible.

Create the service order with advanced execution order item and add the billing relevance as “Summarized Billing” and enter the DIP Profile.

After creating the service order, user must manually release the item. On released action credit check process is getting triggered to check whether the user is having sufficient credit to create the new service order or to release the item.Further integration with the Credit Management process gets trigger and update the credit exposure commitments in the 110 (Service Order & Service Contract) categories.  

Credit commitment exposure in 110 category on service order released.

Plant Maintenance Order (PMO) will be created upon successful release of the advanced execution order item. Manual release of the PMO in transaction via “IW32”.

Posting PMO Confirmations in transaction via IW48 to create the Debit Memo Request. Adding the actual work details in IW48 and saving the entry. Go to DP92 and create the debit memo request (DMR).

Debit Memo Request (DMR) is Created.

Credit commitment exposures should get updated in 410 categories on DMR creation.

Create the Debit Memo.

As user created the debit memo, hence credit commitment exposures will move from 410 to 500 category.

Check the 410 Commitment. As the 410 exposures moved to 500 category on successful creation of debit memo. No value should persist in the 410 category

While creating the debit memo (Invoice),Invoice values will automatically send to accounting. Manual release to accounting not needed but to transfer the credit commitment data to 200 category (Open Invoices) user should follow the below steps:

Credit Management is not updated each time an open item in FI-CA is posted.Instead, FI-CA collects all the data relevant for Credit Management (on business partner level) and hands this information over to Credit Management using mass activity “Transfer Credit Data” (TA FPCM1).

Once user create the invoice (VF01). Entry will get updated to the “DFKKOBLID - Partner Messages for SAP Credit Management Update” Table.

Before user run the transaction to transfer the data to 200 category, Note the earlier commitment: Commitment Value : 246.910,97

Run the transaction “FPCM1”.

Enter the Date ID : The date on which user want to schedule the job to transfer the commitment data.

Run ID                 : Enter the uniquely identifies program run id .

Business Partner : Enter the Business Partner and select the below checkboxes as shown in below image.

Go to logs and the Problem Class : Additional Information

Save the Program Run and Execute the Schedule Program Run.

User will get the Job Scheduling Option: Select one of the option to set the Job.

Once the Program run is Completed. Check the table DFKKOBLID. No entries will exist in the table as Credit data is already moved to 200 category.No entry persists in the table.

Check the Credit Commitment Data in the 200 Category. New records stored in the table DFKKOBLID table moved to 200 category on successful program run.

Check the credit commitment date in 500 category. As all the credit data has been transferred to 200 category, all the credit commitment data removed from 500 category.

Steps to follow when Invoice is cancelled.

Cancel the invoice 90014452.

Cancel Invoice (S1) entry will get updated under transaction history tab.During cancellation of the FI-CA document, FI-CA also stores a “partner message” in table DFKKOBLID.

The ID of the partner message is the ID of the SD billing document.

If TA FPCM1 is executed with the parameters marked in below image , these entries from table DFKKOBLID are considered. This information helps FI-CA to know, which commitments need to be reduced.

Once the Program Run is Completed. Check the credit commitment date in 200 category.  65 USD has been reduced from 246,910.97 USD

This is how  credit management works in advanced execution order item in service order for FICA enabled system.

entry.title: News from SAP Activate - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 and more
entry.content:
We are pleased to announce the availability of the updates to SAP Activate. This release significantly enhances the methodology guidance across a wide range of products and solution-specific content. These updates aim to streamline and improve the implementation process, providing a more seamless experience for our valued users. Here are the key highlights for this release. You can learn more in the detailed sections below:

  • SAP Activate Community Blogs and Activate Minute Videos
  • Updates to SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Updates to SAP Activate Methodology for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Introducing SAP Activate for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Upgrade
  • Updates to SAP Activate for Conversion of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2-system to 3-system landscape
  • Updates to SAP Activate for SAP SuccessFactors
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Test Automation Tool 2502 Release

 

SAP Activate Community | SAP Activate Blogs | SAP Activate Minute Videos

Join the SAP Activate Community to explore valuable content and resources while connecting with SAP partners, clients and internal colleagues by participating in discussions and sharing knowledge.

Make sure to follow the SAP Activate tag to access blog posts like those highlighted here, along with additional insights shared by SAP Activate experts within the community.

Recap of some blog posts from the community:

  • 12 Things that SAP implementation methodology SAP Activate tells you to do by Jan Musil
  • Discover SAP Activate Tips in a Minute with SAP Activate Minute Videos by Christina Galbreath
  • SAP Activate road map viewer Now Accessible via SAP for Me by Chandan Gurav
  • How to Begin an SAP Activate for Transformation to SAP Cloud ALM Project by Bob Byrne
  • Optimize SAP Implementation with SAP Activate Methodology and Guided Implementation in Cloud ALM by Ana Goetz
  • SAP Activate for RISE with SAP: Roadmap Updates & New Content!  by Adi Kamugisha
  • Providing Feedback in SAP Activate road map viewer via SAP for Me by Adi Kamugisha
  • Check out the new SAP Activate one minute video, SAP Activate Parallel Line Roadmap.

 

Updates of SAP Activate for SAP S/HANA Cloud Public Edition in 2502

The SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (3-system landscape) implementation roadmap has been enhanced based on product management input, system functionality changes, and customer feedback. Below are highlights for this release.

  • Updates to several tasks in the Project Management workstream and associated accelerators to refresh the procedures and content.
  • Reworked the Conduct Project Quality Gate tasks at the end of each phase. Updated the quality gate accelerator to prepare for the integration of the checklists into SAP Cloud ALM checklist functionality.
  • Added a new tag to easily identify deliverables and tasks associated with the GROW Adoption Plan.
  • Added two new tasks, Request Deployment Readiness Check for Upcoming Go-Live  and Review Business Processes with the CQC for Process Adoption Service, and updated task Request Knowledge Assessment to better support the services related to the continuous Quality Check program (CQC).
  • Added a new task Verify and Locate Provisioning Email from SAP. The task helps to ensure the Initial Admin locates and reviews the consolidated provisioning email from SAP once the request has been submitted via SAP For Me.
  • Migrated the Road Map viewer to the SAP for Me platform to provide a most consistent user experience with other sap support products. Renamed Roadmap Viewer to Road Map Viewer based on the recommendation from SAP One Voice.

GROW Adoption Plan

GROW Adoption Plan tag providing quick access to key adoption tasks

 

Updates of SAP Activate Methodology for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

The SAP Activate Methodology content for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition continues to be enhanced to help guide the customer through the journey of a new implementation, system conversion, and selective data transition following the SAP Clean Core principles. These journeys are accessible by selecting the appropriate journey tag within the single roadmap in road map viewer or individually in SAP Cloud ALM.

Key highlights include:

  • In coordination with the Regional Implementation Group (RIG), several updates have been made in in the roadmaps pertaining to both System Conversion and Selective Data Transition.
  • For Extensibility in System Conversion Fit-to-Standard for Specific Features, User Experience Design and User Access and Security Planning and Design, new guidance and templates for managing functional specifications were added in the EXPLORE phase.
  • Updates have been made for all presentation guides for User Experience.
  • A new task, Review your Clean Core Assessment, for the Prepare, Explore, Realize, and Run phases under the deliverable Clean Core Quality Gate was added. This task provides guidelines for conducting a comprehensive review of the Clean Core Assessment to ensure alignment with Clean Core principles throughout the project activities.
  • Tasks pertaining to managing the SAP Business Network have been added including Self-Enable on SAP Business Network and Setup Integration for S/4 HANA System and SAP Business Network.

See the release notes for a complete list of all improvements.

 

Introducing SAP Activate for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Upgrade

This roadmap is designed to guide existing Private Cloud Edition customers (both direct and indirect) in transitioning to the latest release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. This enables customers to benefit from the latest innovations, including AI, enhance security and compliance, avoid costs associated with unnecessary custom code, and maximize the lifespan of their systems. As with the other RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition roadmaps, adherence to SAP Clean Core principles is embedded.

The content is already available in the SAP Activate road map viewer and SAP Cloud ALM.  Key pillars are:

Unleash Innovation

  • Increase productivity and improve business processes with advanced Business AI, automation and predictive analytics.
  • Enhance user experience and collaboration with Joule, SAP Fiori and Microsoft Teams integration.
  • Benefit from our continuous investment in business processes and integration technologies to facilitate the interaction in hybrid system environments.

Foster Security and Compliance

  • Stay secure with built-in cybersecurity and data protection based on the latest standards.
  • Improve risk and compliance transparency and always adhere to local and global regulations.
  • Respond to the latest compliance trends.
  • Prevent IT audits issue and be compliant with compatibility packs usage.

Build An Upgrade Routine

  • Optimize the upgrade strategy and benefit from greater flexibility and easier-to-consume innovation with the new release and maintenance strategy.
  • Lower upgrade impact by following SAP's clean core principles that make upgrades a seamless part of the software lifecycle by running on the latest release for regular adoption of innovation.
  • Upskill teams with the Enterprise Support Academy and dedicated SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Value Maps.

Leverage Tools and Services

  • Assess the upgrade impact by running SAP Readiness Check.
  • Reduce efforts and improve quality using Test Automation.
  • Benefit from SAP support guidance and resources included with RISE subscription for the technical upgrade execution.
  • Leverage services and support from SAP and its ecosystem.

Links:

  • RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition – Upgrade
  • Upgrade Community Page
  • White Paper

SAP Activate for upgrading RISE SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

 

Updates of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2-system landscape to 3-system landscape 

The following are recent updates to the content based on insights from customer conversion projects, feedback from internal stakeholders, and customer-like testing activities.

  • Refreshed guidance for planning, receiving, and confirming the Test Data Refresh service. All tasks have been updated, new checklist has been added to reflect the new process for planning the service, preparing for its delivery, and confirming the service results. (link)
  • Updated accelerator Delta Features 2-system landscape to 3-system landscape that details the new capabilities of the 3-system landscape solution. You can access it on this task. (link)
  • Added new accelerator SAP BTP Guidance Framework to task Develop and Transport Side-by-Side Extensions in the Run phase. (link)
  • Updated procedure notes in tasks Confirm Conversion Project Schedule in the Discover phase to make sure Solution Builder customers consider the impact of specific activities on their conversion project. (link)
  • Added a new task Activate Scope Item 5XU to Use Document and Reporting Compliance in the Realize phase to guide customers using Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) features to create, process, and monitor electronic documents and statutory reports post-conversion. (link)
  • Updated task description and accelerators for Grant Access to Partner for Creating and Managing Cases in the Prepare phase. (link)

Refer to the methodology overview page in the SAP Activate road map viewer for the latest content updates and the overview video here. Additionally, bookmark the SAP Community page dedicated to conversion topics here.

 

Updates of SAP Activate for SAP SuccessFactors

The SAP Activate for SAP SuccessFactors roadmap has been enhanced based on product management input, system functionality changes, and customer feedback. Below are the highlights:

  • Updates to several tasks in the Project Management workstream and associated accelerators to refresh the procedures and content.
  • Task changes to improve results when combining with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition road map in SAP Cloud ALM.

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Test Automation Tool 2502 Release

In the 2502 release of the Test Automation Tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, we are enhancing the Post Upgrade Tests (PUT) with a new app and various improvements. Key highlights include:

  • The new Post Upgrade Test (PUT) app offers various dashboards that visualize key factors of PUT execution. Additional enhancements include:
    • One click navigation to all applications used in PUT.
    • The ability to compare results from two Post Upgrade Test (PUT) runs.
    • Enhanced contextual information for Trigger and Tracking PUT runs.
    • Consolidated of all PUT related actions in once place (Consent | Maintain | Roles | Trigger).
  • Improved analytics in the intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS) app provide overall and application area specific scope efficiency for the optimized scope.

For more details, refer to the blog on the 2502 features in the Test Automation Tool.

 

Run simple – SAP Activate – Accelerate and simplify the adoption of SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Your SAP Activate Team

entry.title: Unlocking the Power of Version Management in SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning
entry.content:
Version Management within SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) for Planning is an area that needs to be organized and pristine.  This blog explores the differences between Private and Public versions and how to manage versions effectively using examples from best-practice business content, such as the Rapid FP&A Suite for SAP S/4HANA.

Understanding Private and Public Versions in SAP Analytics Cloud

Private Versions and Public Versions serve distinct purposes in SAP Analytics Cloud:

  • Private Versions are individual workspaces where planners can experiment with scenarios and updates without affecting others. Private Versions allow users to test different planning approaches in isolation.

  • Public Versions are shared, organization-wide Versions that serve as the official source of truth for FP&A data. When a planner inputs data into a Public Version, their inputs are captured in a Private Version. The planner must then actively choose to "publish" their changes so that they appear in the Public Version.

Establish a Single Planning Version

The process within the Rapid FP&A Suite uses a single, write-enabled, public PLAN version—the Working Plan—for all active planning activities. This approach minimizes confusion by avoiding multiple editable Plan Versions simultaneously. Multiple editable Versions (e.g., Plan_1, Plan_2) upfront, can:

  • Confuse planners about where to submit their data.

  • Require constant updates to input forms and associated security settings for each Version.

  • Introduce excessive variability into workflows, leading to errors.

To simplify the process, the SAP consultants behind Rapid FP&A suggest:

  • Focus on a Single PLAN Version: Using a single PLAN Version ensures planners always know where to input their data. This reduces confusion and eliminates the administrative burden of maintaining multiple editable Versions.

  • Pre-Configured Input Forms in Rapid FP&A: Input forms in the Rapid FP&A Suite are pre-configured to filter on the PLAN Version, ensuring planners work in the "Working Plan" Version.

  • Leverage Data Actions: The Rapid FP&A Suite includes pre-configured Data Actions that allow you to copy any PLAN data into finalized, version-specific names like Target2025 or Budget2024. Once copied, these versions become read-only and are naturally referred to by their specific names (e.g., Forecast2025) for clarity and consistency.

Why does this approach work? It focuses planners on completing one version of the Plan before moving to another, avoiding the complexity and errors of managing multiple editable versions simultaneously.

Managing Data Within Private Versions

Private Versions offer a flexible environment to refine and validate planning scenarios before publishing to Public Versions. Here are some best practices:

How to Use Private Versions Effectively

Planners must flow their plan inputs through Private Versions, regardless of how Public Versions are managed, so educating Planners on how to use Private Versions optimally is crucial. Proper use of Private Versions ensures accurate updates to the plan and provides  flexibility:

  • Experiment and Refine: Use Private Versions to model scenarios, refine your inputs, and validate changes without impacting others.

  • Publish with Purpose: Once all updates are finalized, publish the changes to the Public Version to make them available for organizational use. Under the Rapid FP&A Suite, published updates flow into the PLAN Version (Working Plan), which admins can later archive into specific Version names (e.g., Budget2026) once all plans are collected.

  • Utilize Tools for Confidence: Features like the “Revert” function and “Version History Panel” provide a safety net to undo mistakes during refinement of your Private version. Remember: Reverting is unavailable after publishing it in the Public Version.

Tip: The Rapid FP&A Suite includes pre-configured input forms and sample data, allowing you to practice updating and publishing Private Versions in a "practice" environment.

Best Practices for Managing Public Versions

Creating multiple specific Version names (e.g., Budget2026, Forecast2025) at the start of every planning cycle can be problematic. This approach requires updating all input forms and reports to align with each new Version name, creating inefficiencies and potential confusion. Additionally, frequent changes to Version names can leave users unsure where to input their plan data, causing unnecessary delays and errors.

Use Categories to Organize Your Versions

You can use the category property to organize your versions by type. Categories help distinguish versions and streamline management. The following categories can help organize versions:

  • Actual, Budget, Plan, Forecast, Rolling Forecast

A model can have one public Actuals version, typically updated via import from a data source like S/4HANA. However, private copies of this version can be created for analysis or testing. Categories like Forecast allow you to create multiple forecast versions to explore optimistic, baseline, or worst-case outcomes.

Under Best Practices in the Rapid FP&A Suite, planners will focus on one scenario at a time (e.g., baseline) within the PLAN Version. Once the scenario is finalized, it can be archived under a version-specific name such as Baseline2025 or Optimistic2025, and then the planner can move on to the following scenario. This approach ensures planners complete one scenario before moving to another, avoiding confusion and streamlining the process.

Hold Actual Data in a Read-Only Version

  • Store finalized Actual data in a read-only ACTUAL Version. This ensures the integrity of historical data and provides a clear baseline for variance analysis.

Archive Specific Versions for Reporting

  • Instead of constantly updating input forms and security for new write-enabled Versions, take snapshots of the working PLAN Version at key points in the planning cycle. Archive these snapshots into finalized, read-only Versions (e.g., Budget2026, Forecast2025) using pre-built Data Actions from the Rapid FP&A Suite.

  • This approach preserves the integrity of snapshot data while avoiding the repetitive task of reconfiguring templates and security for every user or group needing write access to a plan version.

How Rapid FP&A Suite Supports Version Management

The Rapid FP&A Suite for SAP S/4HANA is a valuable toolset for:

  1. Testing and Experimentation

    • Use sample data to explore how updates to Private Versions flow into Public Versions.

  2. Streamlined Workflows

    • Discover how we maintain a single write-enabled PLAN Version for all planning activities.

    • See how admins can create finalized, locked-down Versions efficiently by copying a version from PLAN

  3. Reducing Administrative Overhead

    • You will see how pre-configured input forms align with the active working PLAN Version.

    • With only one active PLAN version all the time, you simplify security and configuration management

  4. Preserving Data Integrity

    • Finalized Versions like Budget2026 or Forecast2025 are clearly labeled and locked for accurate reporting.

    • Any other interim snapshots of PLAN can be archived for reviews or status meetings.

Benefits for FP&A Teams?

  • Planners: Clarity on where to input data with a single active Version.

  • Admins: Efficiency in managing Versions with reduced maintenance effort.

  • Stakeholders: Reliable, finalized Versions ready for variance analysis and decision-making.

Getting Started

Ready to unlock the full potential of Version Management? Leverage the Rapid FP&A Suite for SAP S/4HANA as a toolset to test and refine your planning processes. Explore its pre-configured content, including input forms, data actions, and sample scenarios, to simplify and standardize your workflows. The best part? You can download the suite in minutes and review these processes using sample data—no integration is required during your discovery phase.

How do you Download the free Rapid FP&A Suite?
Downloading the Rapid FP&A Suite is straightforward and hassle-free.
Follow these steps to get started:
1. Log in to your SAP Analytics Cloud account.   Please don't use a production tenant.
2. Navigate to the "Content Network" on the left-hand side panel of SAC
3. Select "Business Content"
4. Search for "Rapid" in the upper right-hand 'Search' box - you should see Rapid Finance, Workforce, Sales
4. Follow the instructions to complete the download and installation of each of the Rapid modules (Rapid Finance, Rapid Sales, Rapid Workforce).

Let’s make planning Rapid, Simple, and Standard with SAP Analytics Cloud!
